text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Each additional egg per day consumed increases risk of cardiovascular disease by 4% compared with non‑consumers.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"4% increased risk of CVD for each additional egg/day consumed vs. non-consumers.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly quantifies a 4% higher risk of CVD for each extra egg per day relative to non‑consumers, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"each additional egg per day","comparator":"non‑consumers","outcome":"risk of cardiovascular disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"4% increased risk","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"There were no significant associations for cardiovascular disease mortality at egg intakes of 1 per day versus 0.007 per day.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"No significant associations for CVD mortality at egg intakes of 1/day vs. 0.007/day and no DR effect.","entailment":"The quoted sentence states that comparing 1 egg per day to 0.007 eggs per day showed no significant difference in CVD mortality, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"egg intake of 1 per day","comparator":"egg intake of 0.007 per day","outcome":"CVD mortality","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"no significant associations","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Comparing high versus low egg consumption showed no significant associations with risk of mortality for cardiovascular disease, ischaemic heart disease, or stroke.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"No significant associations with risk of mortality for CVD, IHD or stroke when comparing high vs. low egg consumption.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that high versus low egg consumption did not significantly affect mortality risk for CVD, IHD, or stroke, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"high egg consumption","comparator":"low egg consumption","outcome":"mortality risk for CVD, IHD, stroke","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"no significant associations","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Each additional egg per day increases risk of cardiovascular disease mortality by 7%.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"7% increased risk of CVD mortality with each additional egg/day.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states a 7% higher risk of CVD mortality for each extra egg per day, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"each additional egg per day","comparator":"","outcome":"CVD mortality","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"7% increased risk","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"For each additional egg per week, risk of stroke mortality decreased by 4%.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"DR analysis: for each additional 1 egg/week, risk of stroke mortality decreased by 4%.","entailment":"The quoted sentence indicates a dose‑response finding that each extra egg per week lowers stroke mortality risk by 4%, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"each additional 1 egg per week","comparator":"","outcome":"stroke mortality","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"decreased by 4%","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"When comparing highest versus lowest egg intake, there was a 9–12% reduced risk of stroke.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"When comparing highest versus lowest egg intake, there was a 9–12% reduced risk of stroke","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that the highest egg consumers had a 9–12% lower stroke risk compared with the lowest consumers, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"highest egg intake","comparator":"lowest egg intake","outcome":"stroke risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"9–12% reduced risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Consuming up to 3.5 eggs per week was associated with a significant reduction in stroke risk, but this became non‑significant at higher intakes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Consuming up to 3.5 eggs per week was associated with a significant reduction in stroke risk, but this became non‑significant at higher intakes.","entailment":"The quoted sentence describes a significant protective effect of up to 3.5 eggs per week on stroke risk that disappears at higher consumption, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"up to 3.5 eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"stroke risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"significant reduction","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Eating up to 2 eggs per week was associated with a 4% decreased risk of coronary heart disease incidence or mortality.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"eating up to 2 eggs/week was associated with a 4% decreased risk of CHD incidence/mortality","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly links consumption of up to 2 eggs weekly with a 4% lower risk of CHD incidence or mortality, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"up to 2 eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"CHD incidence/mortality","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"4% decreased risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Higher egg consumers (>1 egg per day) had an 11% reduced risk of coronary heart disease compared with lower consumers (≤1 egg per day).","claimType":"empirical","quote":"significant 11% reduced risk when comparing higher consumers (>1 egg/day) to lower (≤1 egg/day)","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports an 11% lower CHD risk for those eating more than one egg daily versus those eating one or fewer, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"higher consumers (>1 egg per day)","comparator":"lower consumers (≤1 egg per day)","outcome":"coronary heart disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"11% reduced risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Intakes of 7 eggs per week were associated with a 15% increased risk of heart failure compared with no consumption.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"intakes of 7 eggs/week was associated with an increased risk of 15%, rising to 23% for 9 eggs/week, when compared with no consumption","entailment":"The quoted sentence indicates that consuming 7 eggs weekly raises heart failure risk by 15% relative to no egg consumption, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"7 eggs per week","comparator":"no consumption","outcome":"heart failure risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"15% increased risk","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"People with non‑specific diabetes eating the highest number of eggs had an 83% increased risk of cardiovascular disease compared with the lowest consumers.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"people with non-specific ‘diabetes’ eating the highest number of eggs had an 83% increased risk of CVD compared with the lowest consumers","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that among individuals with unspecified diabetes, the highest egg consumers faced an 83% higher CVD risk than the lowest consumers, supporting the claim.","population":"people with non‑specific diabetes","intervention":"eating the highest number of eggs","comparator":"lowest consumers","outcome":"cardiovascular disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"83% increased risk","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"For every 4 eggs per week increment, relative risk of cardiovascular disease was 40%
